page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.20xlarge is an x86_64 architecture compute-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated physical cores and 80 vCPUs utilizing the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor, which operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 1.0 thread per core, meaning hyperthreading is disabled, providing dedicated execution resources per vCPU. It includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.4 GB per core.

This server type does not bundle local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a high-density compute resource, trading off local storage and massive memory capacity for dedicated, high-frequency CPU cores.

The ecs.hfc6.20xlarge is designed for compute-heavy workloads that require high single-core performance and dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. Suitable workloads include high-performance computing, intensive batch processing, scientific modeling, and compute-bound application servers that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.20xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 80 dedicated physical cores and 80 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with hyperthreading disabled, it provides 192.0 GB of system memory, resulting in 2.4 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on external storage solutions, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive applications, batch processing, and high-performance computing workloads that benefit from dedicated, high-frequency CPU resources rather than large memory allocations or local storage.
